name: Palm Beach Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Cocoa Powder (plus 1 teaspoon for dusting)
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust an oven rack one-third up from the bottom and preheat the oven to 425 degrees.
2. Line a 9x11x2-inch pan with foil, brushing it well with melted butter.
3. Melt the chocolate and butter in the top of a large double boiler over hot water on moderate heat, or in a small, heavy saucepan on low heat, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat when combined.
4. Break the walnuts into pieces, keeping them fairly large.
5. In a large bowl, beat the eggs with vanilla, almond extracts, salt, espresso powder, and sugar at high speed for 10 minutes.
6. On low speed, add the chocolate mixture and beat only until mixed. Remove the bowl from the mixer.
7. Stir in the nuts.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 35 minutes, reversing the pan from front to back once to ensure even baking. The cake will have a firm crust but appear underbaked when tested with a toothpick.
10. Remove the pan from the oven and let cool completely. Invert the brownies onto a cookie sheet and remove the foil. Cover with waxed paper and another cookie sheet, then invert again.
11. This cake must be covered and refrigerated for a few hours or overnight before cutting into bars.
12. Makes 32 Jumbo Brownies.
